subject: Mozi Minute: One Extra Billing Cycle Per Year
body:
Words I like: When push comes to shove...Cashflow > Profit > Revenue

Mozi minute: An Extra Billing Cycle Per Year

If you run a recurring business.

Bill every 4 weeks instead of every month.

It is a great way to increase profit.

In a year there are 13, four-week cycles.

Compared to 12, one-month cycles.

Meaning you get an extra billing cycle with no extra work.

Front end sales hardly dip from billing every 28 days.

It feels the same as monthly to customers.

But, this creates an 8.3% increase in revenue that drops disproportionately to the bottom line.

Hypothetical example:

Let's say you have 600 customers paying $100 per month.

That's $1,200 per year each.

At a 20% margin, you make $240 in profit per customer.

In total you're doing $720,000 in revenue and $144,000 in profit.

But...

If you bill $100 every 4 weeks instead.

Customers now pay about $1,300 per year at $340 profit.

Total revenue climbs to $780,000.

Profit sits at $204,000 (if fixed costs stay the same.)

Same customers. Same work. $60,000 more profit.

Proof:

We used this at over 5,000 gyms at GymLaunch and saw zero effects in sales.

Most gyms not only had an 8% increase in revenue but significantly greater increases in profit.

As promised, value in under a minute.

Bill every 4 weeks instead of monthly,

- Alex
